_meta.purpose: Authoritative live state for JobTrackr-CV. Read at session start. Update on significant changes.

project.name: JobTrackr-CV
project.summary: AI-assisted job discovery + CV tailoring SaaS. One codebase, one Supabase DB, three services: frontend/web (Vercel), backend/worker (Fly), backend/api (Fly).

web.stack: Next.js 16 (Turbopack) App Router, Tailwind v4, Supabase browser client
web.deploy: git push origin main — auto-deploys to production

worker.notes: Stage-4b filter is title-only + optional teaser rescue (driven by profile.must_include_phrases, migration 046). Primary SEEK: seekDirect (curl_cffi Chrome-124 TLS, no proxy); Apify actor: fallback only via profile.seek_method='actor'. Careerjet: direct HTML scraping via curl_cffi (API abandoned to avoid tracking URL IP-blocks). Adzuna: API teasers by default (profile.adzuna_method='api'); opt-in 'direct' mode scrapes /details/<id> HTML for full JDs (migration 047, cap 50). Full-JD threshold: 1400 chars. Jora: disabled (OOM on 512MB VM).

cv_backend.stack: FastAPI, Python 3.11, ReportLab, Supabase service-role client

build_state.current_phase: phase-13 — monetization: Stripe subscriptions + usage metering (IN PROGRESS, not yet production)
build_state.billing_high_level: Adding paid subscriptions (Stripe, AUD) gating the platform. AI cost is now the platform's (BYOK removed 2026-06-16, see D20) — plan caps/pricing below were set when AI cost sat with the user and need revisiting. Plans: 3-day card-upfront TRIAL (3 tailored CVs + 3 cover letters, 1 profile, 1 run) → WEEKLY (50 unique / 75 total CVs + letters, 5 profiles, 30 runs) → MONTHLY (250 unique / 375 total, 10 profiles, 120 runs) → UNLIMITED (∞, higher price). Total = unique × 1.5. Tailored CV and cover letter are SEPARATE buckets, equal caps (either/or). Caps reset per Stripe billing period (counted by current_period_start). Auto-renew ON (Stripe default; trial auto-converts after 3 days; subscriptions auto-renew each period). On cancel/expiry → READ-ONLY (existing CVs/letters viewable, no new analyses). Existing role='beta' users grandfathered via a comp subscription with a 14-day grace. Enforcement is at the 4 web-layer choke points only (createProfile, profiles/[id]/run, jobs/[id]/analyze, jobs/[id]/cover-letter); cv-backend + worker untouched. Migration 051_billing.sql. Pricing numbers are placeholders to finalize before go-live.
build_state.production_writer: w8_verified (W3/W8+) — now the CODE DEFAULT in config.py (TAILORED_CV_WRITER: str = 'w8_verified'). Also set as a Fly secret on jobtrackr-cv-api for belt-and-braces. Legacy W1 still exists inside W3 as a building block (run_tailored_cv called by _impl.py); it is NOT dead code. Revert via TAILORED_CV_WRITER=legacy Fly secret.
build_state.production_ats_scorer: PRODUCTION (main): S1 v1 deterministic 50/35/15. REFACTOR BRANCH (refactor/architecture-review): ATS v2 = 50 keyword / 40 experience / 10 formatting. Experience score REBUILT from three CV<->JD-alignment sub-signals: responsibility coverage (20, JD duties <-> CV bullets), relevant tenure (12, CV months in JD vertical vs experience_years_required), vertical alignment (8, %% CV entries in JD vertical). Removed v1's two defects: required-keyword double-count + role-family freebie. Tailoring invariant: keyword injection moves Cat 1 only (experience reads the narrative, formatting reads structure - neither fabricable from the feasibility plan, so predicted lift == actual lift). New module app/services/cv/experience_parser.py. min_initial_ats lowered 60->50 to match honest scoring. Full design: backend/api/docs/ATS_SCORING_V2.md.
build_state.last_shipped: 2026-06-23 (tier-gated sources + tracking, commit 86dfca3) — THREE changes on main. (1) SEEK Apify listings fallback gated to unlimited tier only; weekly/monthly use SEEK direct only (no paid fallback). (2) SEEK JD Apify fallback removed entirely — SEEK JD enrichment is free-direct-only for all tiers. Two paid Apify actors total, both unlimited-only: SEEK listings actor + Adzuna JD-fetcher actor. Adzuna actor failure now logs clearly (falling back to API teasers). (3) SOURCE METHOD TRACKING: sourceMethods object built per run (tier, per-source method/enrichment/fallback/counts), persisted to run_logs.source_methods (migration 065 — additive ALTER TABLE). PlatformSourcesCard rewritten as read-only 3-column matrix (Weekly/Monthly/Unlimited); /api/admin/sources route deleted. Admin Sourcing health page: source method health KPIs (SEEK failures, Apify fallbacks, Adzuna actor failures) + Tier + Source methods columns in Recent runs table. PENDING (manual, in order): apply 062→063→064→065 migrations in Supabase SQL editor; flyctl deploy --config backend/worker/fly.toml. PREVIOUS: 2026-06-23 (admin-sources + view-as session, commit f4512de) — THREE changes on main. (1) JOB SOURCES → ADMIN GLOBAL: migration 063 platform_sources (single row, RLS service-role only) holds enabled_sources + adzuna_method + seek_method; admin Integrations page got a PlatformSourcesCard; GET/PATCH /api/admin/sources; orchestrator.loadPlatformSources() OVERRIDES profile.enabled_sources/adzuna_method/seek_method at run time (per-profile columns now vestigial). ProfileForm: entire Sources section deleted (SourceRow/RadioRow/SourceId removed); Schedule renumbered 4, Automation 5. extractSourceFields() in actions/_helpers.ts still writes defaults to vestigial cols — harmless, orchestrator ignores them. (2) EMAIL REMOVED from admin Integrations (users still connect Gmail/Outlook at My CV → Email account). Integrations is now Job sources + Apify quota only. (3) ADMIN 'VIEW AS USER': /api/admin/view-as?mode=user|admin sets/clears jt_user_view cookie. layout reads cookie → renders user nav + fetches user sidebar data; dashboard page skips admin→/dashboard/admin redirect in user-view. SidebarNav: 'View as user' link in admin nav (Preview section); 'Back to admin' banner in user-view. Always the admin's OWN account — not impersonation. PENDING (manual, in order): apply migration 062 (jd_quality 1000) THEN migration 063 (platform_sources) in Supabase SQL editor; flyctl deploy --config backend/worker/fly.toml (covers JD_MIN_USABLE=1000 + fullRefresh + platform-token fallback + loadPlatformSources). PREVIOUS: 2026-06-23 (sources full-JD + JD-threshold session) — Careerjet/Adzuna full-JD investigation + Apify-actor enrichment, on main. (1) CAREERJET: direct HTML scrape is Cloudflare-Turnstile-blocked from every cloud path (verified: datacenter Turnstile-blocks even a real browser; Apify residential gets a 'Verification required' interstitial; even residential+Playwright+warm-up tarpitted). Reverted careerjet to the FREE v4 API (search.api.careerjet.net, IP-whitelisted) for listings (snippet ~251 chars); built a careerjet-jd-fetcher actor (now dormant, gated on CAREERJET_ACTOR_ID); re-ranked careerjet SOURCE_BONUS 1800->300 (below adzuna 400) since it only carries a snippet (winner.ts). (2) ADZUNA full-JD WORKS: /details/<id> 429-rate-limits the Fly IP (Retry-After 3600) but a residential IP returns clean static HTML (section.adp-body). Built adzuna-jd-fetcher actor (CheerioCrawler + Apify RESIDENTIAL AU, no browser); listings stay free via the Adzuna API; stage 7d enriches survivors via enrichAdzunaJDsViaActor when ADZUNA_ACTOR_ID + Apify token present. Verified in prod: '4/4 full descriptions merged'. (3) PLATFORM APIFY TOKEN: loadApifyIntegration() falls back to any founder/admin Apify integration when the profile owner has none (spend tracked on admin row). (4) FULL REFRESH run option (queue->worker->orchestrator, no migration) re-runs the deep 28-day window on demand; RunNowButton shows a 'Full refresh' button beside 'Run now'. (5) PIPELINE CONSOLE persists after a run (LiveLogConsole tracks latest run regardless of status; runs route orders desc). (6) JD THRESHOLD aligned to 1000: jd_quality classifier (migration 062, replaces 050's 1400), analyze JD_FULL_THRESHOLD, worker JD_MIN_USABLE, jobs.ts manual-add — all 1400->1000 to match MANUAL_JD_MIN_CHARS. Apify actors: prospect_fuzz~adzuna-jd-fetcher (live), ~careerjet-jd-fetcher (dormant). PENDING MANUAL: apply migration 062 in Supabase SQL editor; flyctl deploy worker for JD_MIN_USABLE + fullRefresh + platform-token. PREVIOUS: 2026-06-22 (Aurora UI + summary/credential reliability session) — TWO tracks, all on main. TRACK A — AURORA SIGNATURE THEME (frontend, scoped, zero regression to other 5 themes): new dual theme aurora-dark (DEFAULT) + aurora-light (teal+violet accent; Space Grotesk display / Plus Jakarta Sans body / JetBrains Mono numerals; WCAG-AA token pairs), density/text-size control (lib/density.ts), donut tokenised to aurora palette (solid per-slice so legend matches), SOLID teal buttons (gradient reverted per user), custom gradient checkboxes, native <select> color-scheme dark (kills the bright-blue popup), and a broad remap of hardcoded Tailwind light-palette utilities (bg/border/text -50/100/200/600/700/800 across green/amber/red/blue/teal/violet) so every chip/card/callout reads on dark — covering JobEditModal, FeasibilityCard, AnalysisRunClient skill chips, SmartFeed source pills, Voice/Stories, EmailIntegrationCard, FunnelChip, dashboard callouts. See OPS-26..29; docs/aurora-preview.html is a standalone reference. TRACK B — TAILORED-CV / JD RELIABILITY (backend; stamp-then-strip + extraction bugs): (OPS-30) _enforce_company_anchor repairs the PARTIAL cherry-pick (one employer named, other dropped) by splicing the missing employer + present-perfect->past tense; (OPS-31) availability line was stamped then deleted by verify_claims — fixed via verify._collect_summary skip + idempotent re-stamp after verify; (OPS-32) the employer anchor itself was stamped pre-verify then stripped by verify_claims — fixed by re-running _enforce_company_anchor in the post-verify chain of _writer_w8_verified; (OPS-33) credential trim drops trailing prose adjectives + dangling connectors ('Bachelor of Nursing and ready' -> 'Bachelor of Nursing'). Availability also MOVED from Registration & Licences to the END of the Professional Summary (stamp_availability_in_summary). 1167 backend tests pass; tsc + next build clean. PENDING: one more cv-backend deploy (flyctl deploy -a jobtrackr-cv-api) to make OPS-30/31/32/33 fully live. PREVIOUS: 2026-06-21 (My CV session) — "My CV" unified page + build-a-CV-from-scratch feature (commits dd0e74b, 151532a, 7192cf3, f3b6b70, 643d7de, bebab40 on main). (1) Merged My Details + CV Library into one /dashboard/cv page (ProfileDetailsClient: single React-context state + one PATCH to /api/user/preferences to avoid wholesale-replace clobber; old /dashboard/settings/profile redirects here preserving OAuth params; SidebarNav collapsed to one "My CV" item). (2) Build-from-scratch: POST /api/cv/create makes a blank cv_versions row with a built:// sentinel in pdf_storage_path (no migration); CvReviewClient gained mode="create"; built CVs flow through the SAME analysis pipeline via normalized_cv_text (structured PATCH also syncs cv_text for built CVs); ensureActive/DELETE/account-delete guard the built:// sentinel. (3) Vertical-aware skill labels (lib/cv/skillLabels.ts, driven by role_families). (4) AI skill extraction for built CVs: POST /api/cv/[id]/extract-skills -> /internal/categorise-cv, "Suggest from experience" button merges into buckets. (5) Portfolio projects moved from profile to per-CV: StructuredCvProject + ## Projects/custom_sections now rendered by cv_renderer.py (1162 backend tests pass); analyze + resume routes drop the old contact_details.projects merge (prevents double ## Projects). (6) Builder UX: theme-aware bordered inputs, fixed bullet alignment, custom sections, mandatory-field validation with red borders (builder: Experience+Education; profile: Name/Phone/Email/Suburb/State/Postcode + >=1 vertical), two-button Save-as-draft / Save, no autosave in create mode. (7) Misc: forest-green "Reviewed" chip, EmailIntegrationCard text uses theme vars, per-group "Select all / Deselect all" in job board select mode, search-profile save now redirects to /dashboard/profiles (Job Searches) instead of /dashboard. TypeScript + eslint clean on all changed files. PREVIOUS: 2026-06-21 — availability feature + summary/credential/pipeline fixes (all on main). (1) OPT-IN AVAILABILITY chip (PR #7 + follow-ups): profile credentials gained availability[] + show_availability; renders as its OWN italic line under ## Registration & Licences (build_availability_line + stamp_credentials, contact_line.py), off by default, role-gated nursing/manual, JSON-only no migration. See OPS-22. (2) MULTI-ROLE SUMMARY ANCHOR (OPS-23): _ensure_summary_anchors_both_employers generation-time retry in _impl.py _writer_w8_integrated — when CV has 2+ multi-month employers but summary S2 names <2, re-asks model to rewrite S2 into two semicolon clauses naming both; deterministic _enforce_company_anchor 'any()' guard couldn't repair a cherry-picked/award-shaped S2. (3) CREDENTIAL TRIM (OPS-24): _trim_qual_phrase now stops at orphan ')'/sentence period (_CRED_TAIL_TERMINATOR_RE), fixing 'Cert III in Disability or Individual Support). Strong' prose-leak; comma still allowed. (4) WORKER KEYWORD RESCUE (OPS-25): applyKeywordFilter teaser-rescue was dead because it keyed on url_hash (empty until dedup stage) — switched to job.url. CONFIRMED root cause of 'no new jobs since June 8' on profile AIN Sydney (stage 4b dropped 63/76 genuine AIN jobs). (5) DESC-EXCLUSION DIAGNOSTICS: per-phrase breakdown logged at stage 4c/7b (formatExcludeBreakdown) + 100%-drop tripwire WARNs at 4b/4c/7b. Backend 1162 tests green; worker verified via tsx repros (no worker test harness). PENDING DEPLOYS: cv-backend (flyctl deploy --config backend/api/fly.toml) for credential-trim fix; worker (flyctl deploy --config backend/worker/fly.toml) for keyword-rescue + diagnostics. PREVIOUS: 2026-06-20 — JD-analysis pipeline overhaul + tailoring/credential fixes (PR #4, merged to main). Fixes: phantom leadership (weak-grounding token guard), cert-IV satisfies cert-III (AQF subsumption ladder scoped to care family), credential prose-capture eliminated (_trim_qual_phrase stops at parenthetical, component labels routed to noise), award duplication with middle-dot separator fixed, employer extraction handles inline-date headings, anchor injection broadened dangling-clause guard, AHPRA word-boundary fix, CI fix for _apply_setting_bridge re-export. Also merged PR #5 (eslint safe cleanup: prefer-const, dead directives, unused imports). 1111 backend tests passing, TypeScript clean. Phase 13 billing: code-complete (migration 051–061, entitlements, Stripe routes, 4 choke points, billing dashboard, onboarding/plan page) — awaiting Stripe env vars in Vercel to go live. PREVIOUS: 2026-06-19 — professional summary quality fixes (7 commits, all on main, deployed to jobtrackr-cv-api). Root cause: W3 was defaulting to W1 in production (TAILORED_CV_WRITER env not set + code default='legacy'). Fixes: (1) 7c65aa0 — flip code default to w8_verified so W3 is active without relying on a Fly secret. (2) 94920be — TENURE TIEBREAKER in composition.py: adds MULTI-ROLE framing tier so candidates with 2+ multi-month named roles are never hidden behind 'across multiple settings' breadth framing. (3) af0bb6c — _lowercase_generic_care_phrases enforcer: forces 'activities of daily living', 'dementia care', 'electronic medication administration' etc. to lowercase mid-sentence (LLM was Title-Casing them). (4) dc92213 — TITLE SLOT hard example in composition.py: forces opener to use JD job title exactly, not a CV synonym (was writing 'Personal Care Worker' when JD said 'Assistant in Nursing'). (5) c66cbc3 — _enforce_company_anchor: deterministic enforcer that appends 'at Employer1 and Employer2' to S2 when the LLM omits employer names and the CV has 2+ multi-month roles. (6) d3685f9 — guard in _enforce_company_anchor: skips injection when S2 ends with a dangling preposition/conjunction (avoids making an already-broken sentence worse). (7) 1c4be25 — _title_case_role helper: keeps connector words lowercase in the role title ('Assistant in Nursing' not 'Assistant In Nursing'). Also: Fly secret TAILORED_CV_WRITER set to w8_verified on jobtrackr-cv-api. 17 backend tests green. EARLIER 2026-06-14 — structured CV review form v2 (PR #2). 2026-06-10 — JD-analysis quality sweep + summary honesty gates. 2026-06-05 Phase 3B lexicon foundation. 2026-06-02 post-verify skills hygiene + Awards bullet format. 2026-05-30 nursing sub-type labels + cover-letter hardening + adaptive PDF layout.

STILL OPEN THIS SESSION: TRACK 2 — SORT BUG (dashboard + per-profile job boards). Sort options 'date added', 'date posted', 'match score' do nothing — only 'distance' works. Where to look: frontend/web/src/components/jobs/SmartToolbar.tsx (sort dropdown UI + URL param writer) and frontend/web/src/components/jobs/jobFilters.ts (filter/sort applied to the BoardJob array). Likely culprit: SmartFeed's smart-section bucketing (Today's picks / Closest / Fresh today / Needs attention / Everything else) overrides the sort — sections render in bucket order regardless of the user's sort. Per-profile board uses JobTable not SmartFeed, so the bug may be different there. Verify in BOTH boards: dashboard (SmartFeed) and /dashboard/profiles/[id]/jobs (JobTable). Acceptance: changing the Sort dropdown visibly reorders cards on both boards.

build_state.completed_summary: All core phases 0–12 complete: infra, DB migrations (001–048), CV upload, BYOK AI keys, full 9-step analysis pipeline, tailored CV PDF (w8_verified production writer), cover letter generation, Gmail/Outlook OAuth dispatch, applications outbox, voice-directed drafting, analytics funnel, pipeline automation (gate-based auto-tailoring), worker sources (SEEK/Adzuna/Careerjet/Greenhouse/Lever), PipelineDonut (5 lenses), SmartFeed/SmartToolbar, 5-theme system, per-profile source selection, client-side instant filtering on both boards, driving-distance feature.
build_state.active_branch_work: branch refactor/architecture-review (NOT merged to main; user merges later). Carries: (1) JD-extraction quality programme Phases 1-4 (evidence-grounded extraction + groundedness gate; multi-bucket recall floor; subsumption dedup; golden-JD regression harness). (2) ATS scoring v2 (50/40/10 envelope, experience score rebuilt from CV<->JD alignment). (3) Lexicon fixes from real Jesmond AIN re-runs (night-shift/partnership noise, partnership-building + recreational-activities canonicals). 915 tests passing. See backend/api/docs/PHASE_2_PLUS_STATUS.md and backend/api/docs/ATS_SCORING_V2.md.

client_side_filtering.summary: Both dashboard (/dashboard) and profile board (/dashboard/profiles/[id]/jobs) use instant client-side filtering.
client_side_filtering.pattern: Server fetches all ≤200 non-dismissed jobs once, computes progress/pipelineState/atsBand. Client component holds full set and applies filterJobs+sortJobs in useMemo. Stage/triage/sort/keywords use shallowSetParams (History API) — no server round-trip. Location/posted_within/dismissed still hit server.

pipeline_automation.lifecycle: DISCOVERED → JD_CHECK → ROLE_CHECK → INITIAL_ATS_GATE(60) → TAILORED_CV → FINAL_ATS_GATE(70) → COVER_LETTER+EMAIL → APPLICATION_READY → READY_TO_SEND/APPLY → SENT/APPLIED
pipeline_automation.gates_save_cost: JD_CHECK: zero AI cost; ROLE_CHECK: zero AI cost; INITIAL_GATE: saves ~3 AI calls; FINAL_GATE: saves ~2 AI calls
pipeline_automation.cover_letter: Co-generated with email body+subject in ONE AI call. Voice profile (fingerprint + sample) drives register.

billing.summary: Stripe subscriptions (AUD) monetize the platform. Enforcement lives ONLY in the web layer at 4 choke points; backend/api and backend/worker are unaware of billing.
billing.model: Two metered buckets (tailored_cv, cover_letter) + profiles (live count of search_profiles) + runs (per-period event count). consume_usage() Postgres RPC does an atomic cap-check + reservation (FOR UPDATE on subscription). Reserve on trigger (pending) → trigger on analysis_runs/cover_letters status flips to committed (completed) or voided (failed). Dangling pending self-heals after 1h TTL in the count.
billing.entitlement_resolution: founder/admin → unlimited bypass. status='trialing' → TRIAL limits (3/3/1/1) regardless of chosen plan, window = trial period. status='active' → chosen plan limits. status='past_due' → access continues (dunning banner) until Stripe moves to canceled/unpaid. plan='comp' (grandfathered beta) → unlimited until current_period_end (14-day grace) then read-only. canceled/unpaid/no-sub → READ-ONLY + redirect to /onboarding/plan.

structured_cv_review_form.summary: Every uploaded CV passes through a normalising review form before the analysis pipeline consumes it. Removes input variance across thousands of CV layouts. Edits are rearrangement only — verbatim words preserved.
structured_cv_review_form.version: STRUCTURED_CV_VERSION = 4 (frontend/web/src/lib/cvBackend.ts + backend/api/app/services/cv/cv_structurizer.py — bump in lockstep). Review page silently re-runs structurize when stored _version is below this.
structured_cv_review_form.data_flow: Upload → /api/cv (POST) runs structurizeCv + categoriseCv in parallel → merges categoriser skills into structured_cv.skills (mirror) → renders normalized_cv_text via cv-backend /internal/render-canonical-cv → INSERT cv_versions row (cv_text, categorised_skills, structured_cv, structured_cv_status='parsed', normalized_cv_text, is_active iff first upload). Review form PATCH /api/cv/[id]/structured re-renders normalized_cv_text and keeps categorised_skills in lockstep with structured_cv.skills. /api/jobs/[id]/analyze reads normalized_cv_text when present, falls back to cv_text.
structured_cv_review_form.schema_shape: { summary, experience[], education[], awards[], languages[], certifications[], skills{technical,soft_skills,domain_knowledge}, references[], gaps[], _version }. NO contact (profile is source of truth via stamp_contact_line).
bucketing_rules: Care-sector VET quals (Cert III/IV / Diploma in Ageing Support, Individual Support, Community Services, etc.) live in education[] ONLY. Router moves any leaked-into-certifications copy and dedupes by fuzzy key that strips AU unit-code prefix (CHC43015 - X == X).

Awards / Recognition / Honours / Commendations / Achievements / Scholarships → awards[] (not certifications). Recognized by section heading OR by award pattern (X Award, X Scholarship, Recognized for...) even when PDF mashes the heading into adjacent prose.
Languages → own block (preserved but not used by tailored-CV composer).
Experience sorted reverse-chronological by parsed end_date; Present/is_current ranks highest, undated last.
structured_cv_review_form.cv_library_ux: CV cards expand inline (no route change); entire card is the click target with hover-lift; Expand/Collapse button. References card matches the same shell. Active CV is always first. Eager-loaded structured_cv makes expand instant; partial CVs still hit POST /structurize on demand. ensureSomeoneActive() runs on the page load AND on every mutate so the library never shows zero-active when CVs exist.
structured_cv_review_form.application_pool_rule: /dashboard/applications pool requires a COMPLETE set per job: cover letter (status=completed, !stale) + analysis_run (!stale) + tailored CV path (tailored_pdf_storage_path OR tailored_cv_storage_path). Sidebar badge count uses the same rule (frontend/web/src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx) so they cannot diverge. Sent tab unchanged (applied_at NOT NULL AND NOT dismissed).
structured_cv_review_form.tailored_cv_references_format: Plain-text lines, no markdown table. Format: '**Name**, Job title, Company — email@example.com'. Header row 'Referee | Email' and the markdown divider line were removed from build_references_block in backend/api/app/services/cv/contact_line.py.

css_theming.rule: Always use var(--brand), border-border, text-text, bg-surface. NEVER hardcode hex.
css_theming.default: Notion is the project default (getStoredTheme() + the FOUC script in app/layout.tsx fall back to 'notion' when nothing is stored; an explicit 'default' choice is still honoured).
css_theming.public_pages: Landing (app/landing.css scoped to .landing), auth (login/signup inline styles) and privacy do NOT consume theme tokens — they were hand-restyled to the Notion palette (lavender canvas #E6E0F5 / surface #F4EEFB, purple #5645D4 accent, deep purple/navy #241A5C dark sections) + Manrope/Noto Serif. Changing the Notion theme later won't auto-update these.

decisions.decision: Two services — cv-backend stays Python (FastAPI + ReportLab). Preserves 100% of pipeline logic.
Shared Supabase project with production JobTrackr. FK from analysis_runs.job_id → jobs.id requires same DB.
SUPERSEDED 2026-06-16 by D20. (Historical: BYOK Anthropic + OpenAI; no platform AI credits. Zero ongoing cost; user controls spend.)
HMAC-signed internal API between web and cv-backend. Simpler than JWKS; one caller.
Realtime via Supabase postgres_changes on analysis_runs. Same pattern as cv-magic; no polling tax.
Stale strategy: new run on (user, job) marks prior runs is_stale=true. Re-run is user-triggered only.
SUPERSEDED 2026-06-16 by D20 — see below. (Historical: preferred AI provider was stored in localStorage, per-browser, no DB.)
Dropdowns inside overflow:hidden use createPortal + position:fixed + getBoundingClientRect.
Cover letter: single rich AI call + verbatim voice sample + intent rubric + anti-patterns. Replaces 3-pass pipeline that produced mechanical output.
Monetize the platform via Stripe subscriptions (AUD), NOT AI tokens — BYOK stays for now. Subscription bounds infra/Apify cost (job discovery, scraping, pipeline orchestration).

SUPERSEDED 2026-06-16 by D20. (Historical: BYOK was transitional — planned removal in the future, platform would supply AI keys. When removed, AI cost becomes the platform's and plan caps/pricing must be revisited — that revisit is now live, see D20.)
Prioritise direct scraping (seekDirect / Adzuna direct / Careerjet HTML) over the Apify actor to cut per-run cost — actor remains fallback only. Reinforces why runs are capped per plan.

Billing enforcement is web-layer-only at 4 choke points (createProfile, run, analyze, cover-letter). cv-backend + worker stay billing-unaware. consume_usage() RPC + DB triggers are the authoritative meter (reserve/commit/void).
On subscription cancel/expiry → READ-ONLY (existing artifacts viewable, no new work) rather than hard-lock. Grandfather existing beta users with a 14-day comp subscription.

Summary honesty is enforced at GENERATION time in the composer prompt, NOT by deterministic post-passes. 2026-06-16: deleted the dead summary-enforcement code that ran only in tests and never in production — app/services/eval/writers/summary.py (enforce_summary_concreteness + ~14 helpers), enforce_summary_years_gate + enforce_summary_word_floor + _find_summary_block in honesty_guard.py, the dead _strip_canned_summary_phrase/_CANNED_SUMMARY_RE in bridges.py, and their tests (test_sprint_e_summary.py, test_summary_honesty_fixes.py, two test classes in test_honesty_guard.py). Their intent (no inflated years claim, must name employer/scope/metric, 35-word floor) already lives in the production prompt's CAREER-STYLE SUMMARY block. assess_honesty_risk stays — it IS live (logged signal at _impl.py). Net −~1,500 LOC; one source of truth for summary behaviour = the prompt.

BYOK removed 2026-06-16. Single platform-wide AI provider/key/model, admin-controlled, replaces per-user user_integrations rows for anthropic/openai/deepseek. New table public.platform_ai_settings (one row per provider, partial unique index enforcing exactly one is_active=true). Every consumer route (analyze, resume, cover-letter, company-research, stories/extract, voice-profile, cv upload/recategorise/extract-references, email-draft) now calls frontend/web/src/lib/ai/activeProvider.ts getActiveAiCredentials() instead of decrypting a per-user key. Admin manages it at /dashboard/admin/ai-settings (component PlatformAiSettings.tsx, API route /api/admin/ai-settings, role gate founder|admin). Default models centralised in frontend/web/src/lib/ai/models.ts (openai default gpt-5.1, anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6, deepseek deepseek-chat) and mirrored in backend/api/app/services/ai/client.py _DEFAULT_MODELS — keep both in sync by hand, no shared codegen. AI cost is now the platform's; plan caps/pricing must reflect this (ties into D15/D18 billing enforcement, still web-layer-only).

operational_notes.title: TWO tailored-CV prompts: composition.py is PRODUCTION, tailored_cv.py is LEGACY fallback
operational_notes.detail: The orchestrator (app/services/pipeline/orchestrator.py ~line 346) branches on get_settings().TAILORED_CV_WRITER == 'w8_verified'. Production sets the w8_verified Fly secret, so the LIVE path is run_tailored_cv_w8_verified → app/services/eval/writers/_impl.py _writer_w8_integrated → system prompt built by build_composition_system(role_family, seniority) from app/services/ai/prompts/variants/composition.py (the CAREER-STYLE SUMMARY block lives at composition.py ~lines 278-396). The OTHER prompt, app/services/ai/prompts/TAILORED_CV_SYSTEM in prompts/tailored_cv.py, is the LEGACY writer (run_tailored_cv in pipeline/steps/tailored_cv.py) used ONLY when the w8 flag is off. When changing tailored-CV / summary GENERATION behaviour that must show up in the UI, edit composition.py — editing tailored_cv.py alone has NO production effect. composition.py is the most behaviour-sensitive file in the repo (tuned by the real-test loop + golden-JD harness); change it deliberately and re-run the golden tests.

operational_notes.title: Career Highlights word-count: 3-way ceiling contradiction fixed (35-50 single source of truth)
operational_notes.detail: User reported summaries shipping under 25 words despite the composer prompt's '35-50 words HARD MINIMUM' rule. Root cause: the deterministic pipeline only had a MAX-word trimmer (_enforce_career_highlights_words in pipeline/steps/tailored_cv.py, default max_words=65) and an advisory-only validator gate (_gate_profile_word_count in pipeline/steps/tailored_structural_validation.py, pass-band 35-60/absolute-max 80) that the orchestrator logs but never acts on — nothing ever raised a too-SHORT summary back up. Fix has two parts: (1) added app/services/eval/writers/_impl.py:_ensure_career_highlights_floor(), a one-shot corrective retry wired into _writer_w8_integrated right after to_canonical() and before _enforce_structure() — if Career Highlights is below 35 words it asks the model to expand with CV-grounded facts only, accepting the retry only if it strictly improves on the original word count (falls back to original on any failure/non-improvement, never loops). (2) aligned all three previously-inconsistent numeric thresholds (50 prompt / 65 trimmer / 60-80 validator) to the same 35-50 target band: trimmer max_words default 65→50 (tailored_cv.py), validator pass-band 35-60→35-50 with absolute hard-fail moved 80→65 (tailored_structural_validation.py). Test tests/test_tailored_cv_step.py::test_enforce_career_highlights_default_cap_is_65 renamed/updated to assert 50. Also merged the years/seniority rule that was stated independently at composition.py ~285-288 (S1 framing), ~368-370 (seniority-adjective ban), and ~392-396 (SENIORITY WORDS) into one authoritative SENIORITY WORDS block; the other two locations now just point to it. Full suite: 960 passed after all edits.

operational_notes.title: Profile availability is an opt-in italic line under Registration & Licences, not a CV section
operational_notes.detail: Profile credentials JSON gained two fields (contact_details.credentials.availability: subset of ["Full Time","Part Time","Casual"]; show_availability: boolean). When show_availability is true AND >=1 type is ticked, build_availability_line (backend/api/app/services/cv/contact_line.py) yields 'Available: ...' text (canonical order via _AVAILABILITY_ORDER) which stamp_credentials renders as its OWN italic markdown line (*Available: ...*) under the ## Registration & Licences heading, separated from the licences line by a blank line. Deliberately NOT a new CV section, so the tailored-CV writer/composition.py is untouched (zero regression risk); availability is also excluded from build_credentials_line (the licences line stays clean). Off by default. Role-gated to nursing+manual. PDF italic via pdf_generator.py _md_inline *...*→<i>. UI: Availability control lives in the ACTIVE unified My CV component frontend/web/src/components/cv/ProfileDetailsClient.tsx CredentialsSection (shown when nursing||manual). NOTE: an earlier copy was added to ProfileSettingsClient.tsx, but the unified My CV page (commit dd0e74b) renders ProfileDetailsClient and only imports TYPES from ProfileSettingsClient — so the card had to be ported there (this was a real 'I don't see availability' gap). Sanitised/whitelisted in frontend/web/src/app/api/user/preferences/route.ts. JSON-only — no migration. Tests in test_credentials_stamp.py. Initial chip version shipped via PR #7 (squash-merged to main 2026-06-21); moved to its own italic line in a follow-up on main.

operational_notes.title: MULTI-ROLE summary anchor gap: deterministic net can't repair cherry-picked S2 — fixed with a generation-time retry
operational_notes.detail: Bug: when a CV has 2+ multi-month (nameable-anchor) employers, composition.py requires summary S2 to name BOTH (two semicolon clauses). The model sometimes names only one (e.g. the employer that gave an award) and drops the other. The deterministic net _enforce_company_anchor (pipeline/steps/tailored_cv.py:695) could NOT catch this: its 'already anchored' guard uses any(emp in prose for top2) — so a single named employer short-circuits it — and its regex append cannot restructure an award-shaped/partially-anchored S2 into two clauses (would double-name). Fix (per D21, honesty enforced at GENERATION time): added _ensure_summary_anchors_both_employers in app/services/eval/writers/_impl.py — a one-shot corrective retry wired into _writer_w8_integrated right AFTER _ensure_career_highlights_floor and BEFORE _enforce_structure. It reuses _extract_employers_from_cv; when 2+ anchors exist but <2 are named in the summary, it re-asks the model to rewrite S2 into two method+outcome clauses naming both (mixed tense, no tool names, no award-as-S2). Accepts the rewrite ONLY if it now names both top-2 employers AND stays exactly 2 sentences; otherwise keeps the original. Never loops, never fabricates. The deterministic _enforce_company_anchor still runs afterwards as a final no-op safety net. Tests: tests/test_summary_anchor_retry.py (5). Full suite 1160 green. NOTE: _ensure_summary_anchors_both_employers does NOT change the title-slot rule — opener must still equal the JD job title exactly (separate enforcer _enforce_summary_opener).

operational_notes.title: Credential prose-capture: trim now stops at orphan ')'/sentence period, not just '('
operational_notes.detail: Bug: JD credential extraction surfaced 'Cert III in Disability or Individual Support). Strong' — trailing prose leaked into the credential. Root cause in app/services/skills/post_process.py _trim_qual_phrase: the tail walk only broke on a token STARTING with '(' (the '(or equivalent)' case) plus a small prose stop-word whitelist. Malformed JD text with an ORPHAN closing paren (')' with no matching '(') and prose like 'Strong …' had nothing to break on, so it leaked. Fix: added _CRED_TAIL_TERMINATOR_RE = [).;:] — when a tail token contains a closing paren / sentence period / ';' / ':', keep the clean prefix before it, drop the rest, stop. Comma is deliberately NOT a terminator (real names like 'ageing, home and community'). Existing '(or' break + prose stop-words unchanged. Tests in tests/test_skills_post_process.py (test_trim_qual_phrase_*). Full suite 1162 green. NOTE: production cv-backend on Fly (jobtrackr-cv-api) must be redeployed (flyctl deploy) for the fix to affect live JD analyses; the rest of the displayed match output (60/60% Care/Soft, WWCC+NDIS missing, Cert IV⊒Cert III via AQF subsumption) was correct.

operational_notes.title: Teaser-rescue keyword filter was silently disabled (url_hash empty at stage 4b)
operational_notes.detail: Bug in backend/worker/src/pipeline/keywordFilter.ts applyKeywordFilter: the teaser-rescue path (active when profile.must_include_phrases is set) built its 'already passed by title' set from job.url_hash. But url_hash is only assigned later in the dedup stage (normalise() sets it to ''), so at stage 4b every job's url_hash is ''. Result: when ANY title matched, passedHashes={''} → titleRejected=jobs.filter(j=>!has(''))=[] → teaser rescue never ran, so jobs whose TITLE lacks the phrase but whose DESCRIPTION contains it (the whole point of smart-filter rescue) were dropped. Fix: key the passed-set on job.url (canonical URL, already populated by normalise at this stage) instead of url_hash. Verified via tsx repro: a 4-job set with 1 title-match + 2 desc-only matches went from 1 kept (buggy) to 3 kept (fixed). NOTE: only affects profiles using must_include_phrases. Diagnosing a specific 'no new jobs saved' report: each run already logs per-stage kept/dropped counts to run_logs.log_lines (visible in the LiveLogConsole) — 'stage 4b keyword filter: X kept, Y dropped' and 'stage 4c smart filter: ... title missing required / title excluded / desc excluded' pinpoint which gate (and which user rule) zeroes the run. Worker must be redeployed to Fly (flyctl deploy --config backend/worker/fly.toml) for the fix to take effect. CONFIRMED LIVE on profile 2bab2daa (AIN Sydney) via run_logs.log_lines: a 76-job run went stage 4b 76->13 (rescue bug dropped 63 genuine AIN jobs whose title wasn't the exact must_include phrase), then stage 4c 13->2 and stage 7b 2->0 via the user's 'Description must NOT contain' list (home care / Acute Care boilerplate). Rescue fix is the PRIMARY recovery (brings back the 63); desc-exclusion is a secondary trim the user can relax. ALSO ADDED (same commit set): per-phrase attribution on desc-exclusion (excludeByDescription now returns byPhrase; formatExcludeBreakdown renders '[home care: 8, acute care: 3]') logged at stage 4c and 7b, plus 100%-drop tripwire WARNs at stages 4b/4c/7b so a future all-dropped run names the offending rule in the log instantly.

operational_notes.title: Aurora — signature dual theme (dark hero + light twin) + density control
operational_notes.detail: New, original design language added as TWO themes: 'aurora-dark' (default) + 'aurora-light'. 100% additive + scoped — existing 5 themes byte-for-byte unchanged, pipeline/analysis/workflow untouched (CSS + theme registry + fonts only). Signature = teal (--brand) + violet (--violet) 'aurora' gradient, Space Grotesk display headings, Plus Jakarta Sans body, JetBrains Mono numerals, layered elevation, ambient canvas glow. All token pairs WCAG-AA verified (ratios documented in globals.css). Files: (1) globals.css — two :root.theme-aurora-* token blocks + premium component polish scoped to .theme-aurora-* (buttons=aurora-grad primary, fields, .glass/.kpi-card elevation + gradient accent line + mono value, table hover, left-bar sidebar+glow, selection, ambient body glow) + density (--ui-scale on html/body). (2) lib/themes.ts — registered both (Theme type, THEMES with previews, VALID_IDS, THEMED_CLASSES); getStoredTheme default flipped 'notion'->'aurora-dark'. (3) layout.tsx — next/font Plus_Jakarta_Sans/Space_Grotesk/JetBrains_Mono (--font-jakarta/-grotesk/-jbmono; jakarta+grotesk preload, mono lazy; manrope/noto flipped to preload:false as no longer default); FOUC default 'notion'->'aurora-dark' + regex incl aurora-* + density attr. (4) NEW lib/density.ts + components/DensityPickerClient.tsx ('Text size' Compact/Comfortable/Spacious, localStorage 'jobtrackr-density', FOUC-applied). (5) settings/theme/page.tsx — added density picker. Default density 'comfortable' = --ui-scale 1 = no change for other themes. Public pages (landing/auth) unaffected — full-bleed with own palettes. Charts (PipelineDonut canvas, PipelineFunnel) auto-adapt via tokens; their fixed slice hexes already read on dark. Verified: tsc clean + next build exit 0 (fonts resolve, Tailwind compiles). Standalone aurora-preview.html exists at repo root (delivered to user, NOT committed). Known minor: a few callout chips use hardcoded Tailwind light palettes (bg-amber-50 etc.) — readable on dark but not token-themed; future token sweep optional.

operational_notes.title: Credential trim — drop trailing prose adjectives + dangling connectors ('Bachelor of Nursing and ready')
operational_notes.detail: Recurring credential prose-capture (follows OPS-24). JD credential extraction surfaced 'Bachelor of Nursing and ready' (and other run-to-run tails) instead of 'Bachelor of Nursing'. _trim_qual_phrase (skills/post_process.py) walked the tail after _QUAL_PATTERN and only stopped on a small prose set + paren/period terminators — 'and'/'ready' passed through. Fix: (1) expanded _CRED_PROSE_TAIL_STOP with prose adjectives/verbs (ready, able, available, eager, keen, enthusiastic, willing, interested, ideally, preferred, desirable, essential, plus, advantageous, looking, seeking, wanting, equivalent, must, should); (2) added _CRED_TAIL_CONNECTORS and a post-walk loop that strips trailing connector/filler tokens (and/or/with/for/to/in/of/a/an/the/&/...) so we never leave a dangling 'Nursing and'. Legit '...and Community' continuations are preserved (the stop fires on the prose word, not on 'and' itself; the connector-strip only trims the LAST token). Verified: 'bachelor of nursing and ready[ to start]' → 'bachelor of nursing'; 'certificate iv in ageing, home and community care' unchanged; OPS-24 orphan-paren case unchanged. Test test_trim_qual_phrase_drops_trailing_prose_adjectives_and_connectors. Full suite 1167. Deploy: cv-backend (flyctl deploy -a jobtrackr-cv-api).

operational_notes.title: Employer anchor stamped-then-stripped by verify_claims — re-run anchor net post-verify
operational_notes.detail: Same stamp-then-strip class as OPS-31 (availability), now for the SUMMARY EMPLOYER ANCHOR. Symptom (Rashmi, post-deploy): S2 = 'Delivers safe, high-quality care, including electronic medication support for residents living with dementia.' — ZERO employers named despite the CV having Uniting + The Jesmond Group. Root cause: _enforce_company_anchor runs mid-pipeline inside _enforce_structure (_impl.py:654, pre-verify); verify_claims (line 1064) then rewrites the summary and drops the employer name(s), and the anchor net was NEVER re-applied afterward. Fix: re-run _enforce_company_anchor(verified_md, cv_text) in the post-verify chain (imported into _impl.py; placed just before the availability re-stamp). It is idempotent — no-op when both top-2 employers are already named, ZERO-named → append 'at E1 and E2.', PARTIAL → splice missing employer (OPS-30 logic). _find_summary_block matches 'Professional Summary' (the post-verify heading) so the re-run resolves the section correctly. Full suite 1166 passes. Deploy: flyctl deploy --config backend/api/fly.toml. NOTE the user's pasted example was from a deploy predating OPS-30/31/32 — this commit is the systemic fix that makes the anchor survive verify.

operational_notes.title: Availability line was stamped then stripped by verify_claims — re-stamp + verify-skip + idempotency
operational_notes.detail: Root cause of 'availability not appearing on tailored CV' (user-diagnosed, confirmed in code): stamp_availability_in_summary runs mid-pipeline inside _writer_w8_integrated (_impl.py:747), but the w8_verified entry point then calls verify_claims (_impl.py:1064) + a long post-verify re-pass chain that NEVER re-stamps availability. verify._collect_summary bundled the italic '*Available: …*' line into the summary prose (its bullet guard skips '* ' but the note starts '*A' with no space), so when the summary was repaired the line was deleted before storage — affecting BOTH the .md preview and the PDF (same stored file). THREE fixes: (1) verify.py _collect_summary now skips lines matching ^\*?\s*available: (regex) so the verifier never treats availability as a fact-claim. (2) _impl.py run_tailored_cv_w8_verified re-stamps availability LAST (after verify + all re-passes, before result.tailored_md=verified_md). (3) stamp_availability_in_summary made IDEMPOTENT — strips any pre-existing availability line (and the blank we inserted before it) before re-appending, so the double call never duplicates and produces stable output. Tests: test_availability_stamp_is_idempotent + existing summary tests. Full suite 1166 passes. Deploy: flyctl deploy --config backend/api/fly.toml. (UI copy fix 'Professional Summary' already shipped in OPS-30.)

operational_notes.title: Anchor enforcer — partial cherry-pick: splice missing employer + past-tense fix
operational_notes.detail: Symptom from a v370-deployed run (Rashmi): summary S2 = 'Provide person-centred care… at Uniting, and have served as a primary Medication Assistant.' — one employer (Uniting) named, second (The Jesmond Group) dropped. _enforce_company_anchor's original any()-guard treated this as 'already anchored' and skipped. Fix: enforcer now handles THREE cases — (a) BOTH named → no-op; (b) ZERO named → append 'at E1 and E2.' (legacy); (c) PARTIAL → splice the missing employer into a trailing ', and (have) verb-ed' tail (= the cherry-picked second-role clause) AND convert 'have served' (present-perfect) → 'served' (simple past) per the completed-role tense rule. When no such tail exists, append a fresh '; at <missing>.' clause. Module-level _DANGLING_END_RE moved out of the function. New regression tests in test_tailored_cv_step.py: test_enforce_company_anchor_partial_cherry_pick_splices_missing_employer (Rashmi's exact case) + test_enforce_company_anchor_partial_appends_when_no_tail. Full suite 1165 passes. ALSO updated frontend Availability hint text in ProfileDetailsClient.tsx (stale 'under Registration & Licences' → 'at the end of your Professional Summary') to match OPS-26 stamping behaviour. tsc clean, next build exit 0. Deploy needed: flyctl deploy --config backend/api/fly.toml.

operational_notes.title: Aurora readability round 3 + availability moved into Professional Summary
operational_notes.detail: UI: (1) Skills-by-category chips (AnalysisRunClient) + ATS count badge were hardcoded bg-[#DDF4FF] → now bg-[var(--brand)]/10 text-[var(--brand)] border-[var(--brand)]/25 (readable on dark + light, matches the matched/missing keyword chip style). (2) FeasibilityCard TONE_CHIP/TONE_REWRITE + inline violet/amber hexes (#DDF4FF/#FBEFFF/#FFF8C5/#8250DF/#9A6700) → token-based (var(--brand|purple|amber)/12 chips, /8-tint + text-text rewrite boxes) so the 'Suggested rewrite' / inject boxes are readable on dark. (3) Native <select> got color-scheme:dark (aurora-dark) / light so the OS option popup stops showing the bright-blue browser-default highlight. (4) gh-btn-primary reverted from aurora-grad to SOLID var(--brand) so Apply now / Add job / Try-again match the solid-teal Analyze + Save buttons. (5) Voice 'Recommended — writing fresh…' guidance text forced to text-text/text-2 (white) per request. BACKEND (CV render): availability MOVED from the ## Registration & Licences block into the Professional Summary — new contact_line.stamp_availability_in_summary() appends '*Available: …*' (italic) right after the summary paragraph, above the next section; stamp_credentials no longer emits it; wired in _impl.py after stamp_credentials. Tests updated (test_credentials_stamp.py). Verified tsc 0, next build 0, 1163 backend tests pass. NOTE source badges (seek/adzuna=badge-blue, careerjet=badge-teal) already adapt to teal under aurora tokens; screenshots showing blue were pre-deploy.

operational_notes.title: Aurora UI polish — revert gradient buttons/donut, fix native selects + hardcoded light-mode tints app-wide
operational_notes.detail: Round-two consistency pass per user review. (1) REVERTED the broad button[bg-[var(--brand)]]→aurora-grad selector — primary buttons now stay SOLID teal (per user 'lets keep the button green as previously'). Aurora gradient is reserved for KPI card accent + visual identity only. (2) REVERTED donut gradient-ring (removed --donut-grad-* tokens, removed ringGrad/gradStops code in PipelineDonut.tsx); back to per-slice solid colours but in the Aurora palette so legend dots MATCH the donut: Sourcing = teal/violet/cyan (was teal/slate/orange), other lenses use the same family. ATS keeps amber/red for above/below-threshold meaning. (3) Defined --input-border + --input-bg in base :root (were referenced in VoiceCaptureClient textarea but undefined → invisible box). Also voice textarea text-black → text-text so caret/typed text is visible on dark. (4) Custom-styled native <select>: appearance:none, surface-2 background, brand-tinted SVG chevron, options use surface bg + brand-tinted :checked highlight (fixes the bright-blue 'Yes' default browser highlight in AHPRA/work-rights dropdowns). (5) Hardcoded LIGHT-MODE Tailwind tints used app-wide (emerald-50/green-50/amber-50/red-50/blue-50/pink-50 + 200-border + 600/700/800 text) re-mapped under .theme-aurora-* via color-mix(brand/green/amber/red/violet, surface) so EVERY callout/guidance/connected box is readable on dark — fixes Voice 'Write a sample' green box, Voice 'Paste a cover letter' tab guidance, Email 'Gmail connected' (text was white-on-green → now contrast), ProfileForm FunnelChip (#DDF4FF light blue → brand-tinted), Voice AI-phrase amber pills, dashboard thin-JD/passed/ready callout chips. (6) ProgressDots icons next to Analyze button (text-blue-600/purple-600/amber-600/green-600) remapped to brand/violet/amber/green tokens so they're visible on the deep canvas. (7) SelectAll 'Select all/Deselect all' button gets a .data-select-all hook + scoped rule = brand-filled chip matching the Select-mode toggle next to it. tsc clean + next build exit 0.

operational_notes.title: Aurora consistency pass — borders, gradient buttons, checkboxes, chart colours
operational_notes.detail: Follow-up to OPS-26 fixing review gaps where inline-Tailwind/native markup ignored the theme-scoped class CSS. All fixes are token/selector-level in globals.css (scoped to .theme-aurora-*) + PipelineDonut tokenisation. (1) BORDERS: --card-border/--card-bg were referenced (VoiceCaptureClient, StoriesClient, voice/page) but NEVER defined anywhere → border-color fell back to currentColor (a bright border on dark). Defined them in base :root = var(--border)/var(--surface) (fixes all themes). Also tinted aurora --border/--border-muted to a consistent brand-tinted hairline via color-mix so every box+input agrees. (2) BUTTONS: primary brand buttons use inline bg-[var(--brand)] not the .gh-btn-primary class, so added a scoped attribute-selector rule giving button/a[class*='bg-[var(--brand)]']:not(rounded-full) the --aurora-grad gradient + hover lift app-wide; rounded-full pills excluded (keep solid teal). (3) CHECKBOXES: native, unthemed → custom appearance:none gradient box + SVG check (check colour = brand-fg per mode) applied to ALL input[type=checkbox]; radios get accent-color:var(--brand). (4) CHARTS: PipelineDonut slice colours were hardcoded hex → moved to CSS vars (--chart-pos/info/applied/warn/amber/danger/neutral), defaults preserve old palette, aurora overrides to teal/violet/cyan; added resolveColor() so canvas fillStyle resolves var()→hex (DOM legend dots use var() directly). PipelineFunnel already token-driven (no change). Verified tsc 0 + next build 0.

operational_notes.title: Migration tracking is CLI-untracked
operational_notes.detail: All migrations applied manually via Supabase SQL editor. If supabase link is ever set up, run supabase migration repair to mark all migrations as applied before using CLI workflows.

operational_notes.title: bullmq + ioredis in frontend/web/package.json are legitimate
operational_notes.detail: Used by frontend/web/src/app/api/profiles/[id]/run/route.ts and frontend/web/src/lib/actions.ts for BullMQ queue trigger. Server-side only, do not remove.

operational_notes.title: Scrape pipeline observability: run_logs.current_stage + log_lines
operational_notes.detail: current_stage written by setStage() at each transition, cleared on finishRunLog. log_lines (jsonb) streams console output via append_run_log_line() Postgres RPC. UI: LiveRunStatus banner + LiveLogConsole (collapsible). RunNotifier toast in dashboard layout. Polling: LiveRunStatus 3s/12s, LiveLogConsole 4s+1.5s, RunNotifier 3s.

operational_notes.title: curl_cffi bypasses Cloudflare from Fly datacenter without proxy
operational_notes.detail: Python curl_cffi impersonating Chrome 124 TLS bypasses Cloudflare from Fly.io IPs. SEEK + Careerjet return HTTP 200. TypeScript spawns python3 scripts/fetch_jd.py as subprocess; JSON on stdout. Subprocess overhead ~200ms — negligible vs HTTP round-trip.

operational_notes.title: Careerjet API replaced with direct HTML scraping due to jobviewtrack.com blocks
operational_notes.detail: Careerjet's API uses jobviewtrack.com for click tracking, which strictly blocks Fly.io Datacenter IPs with 404s. To avoid relying on an Apify residential proxy, the adapter was rewritten (2026-05-27) to scrape careerjet.com.au HTML directly using curl_cffi. WARNING: HTML scraping is fragile. If the DOM changes or Careerjet strictly rate-limits Fly's IP under high load, the documented fallback plan is to revert to the API adapter and use an Apify Residential proxy.

operational_notes.title: Auth is password-only; master admin account
operational_notes.detail: Magic-link (signInWithOtp) removed from /auth/login 2026-05-25 — email+password only. Master admin: admin@jobtrackr.com (role 'founder' in public.users). Password NOT stored here; set/rotate via Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Users (Add user, Auto Confirm). New auth users get a public.users row automatically via the on_auth_user_created trigger (default role 'beta'); promote with: update public.users set role='founder' where id=(select id from auth.users where email='admin@jobtrackr.com'). Admin route pages (/dashboard/admin, /dashboard/admin/metrics) still exist but are unlinked from the sidebar.

operational_notes.title: Never import runtime values from a 'use client' module into a server component
operational_notes.detail: Next.js returns a client-reference proxy (not the real value), so arithmetic → NaN and the render crashes. This caused the instructions ?step=N 'This page couldn't load' bug. Fix pattern: keep shared constants/data in a neutral plain module (e.g. frontend/web/src/lib/setupSteps.ts) that both server and client import.

operational_notes.title: Dashboard empty state has two modes (gate = !hasAnyJob)
operational_notes.detail: frontend/web/src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/page.tsx: when no jobs exist, coreComplete = status.profile && status.cv && status.aiKey. !coreComplete → FirstRunScreen (Get-set-up wizard / SetupCards). coreComplete → ReadyToScanScreen (create/run a search-profile CTA + the How-it-works deck). The How-it-works deck is the shared component frontend/web/src/components/onboarding/HowItWorksDeck.tsx (built on SwipeDeck.tsx) reused by both this screen and the instructions 'How it works' tab.

operational_notes.title: Profile Location field uses Google Places via a server-side proxy
operational_notes.detail: frontend/web/src/app/api/places/autocomplete/route.ts (auth-gated GET ?q=) calls the Places Autocomplete API (New) at places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete, restricted includedRegionCodes:['au'] + includedPrimaryTypes:['(cities)']. Key is PLACES_API_KEY (server-only, NOT NEXT_PUBLIC_) so it never reaches the browser. UI: frontend/web/src/components/LocationAutocomplete.tsx — controlled input + 300ms-debounced dropdown, still free-typeable (Remote etc.), strips trailing ', Australia' on select to keep the 'Sydney NSW' shape the worker sources expect; replaces the plain input in ProfileForm.tsx. Requires 'Places API (New)' enabled in GCP (distinct from legacy Places API) + billing. Do NOT add an HTTP-referrer key restriction — calls are server→Google from Vercel IPs; an API restriction (limit to Places API New) is fine.

operational_notes.title: Platform-wide AI provider replaces BYOK — migration 060 not yet applied
operational_notes.detail: Migration shared/supabase/migrations/060_platform_ai_settings.sql creates public.platform_ai_settings, best-effort copies any existing founder/admin BYOK key from user_integrations, picks one active row, then DELETEs all anthropic/openai/deepseek rows from user_integrations (irreversible from the app). RISK flagged by migration-checker: if no founder/admin had a previously-valid key, the seeded 'openai' row is left with status=NULL/no key, so NO user's analyses work until an admin opens /dashboard/admin/ai-settings and pastes+validates a key. Required steps before applying: (1) read-only check that a founder/admin user_integrations row has status='valid' and a key for at least one provider; (2) optionally snapshot user_integrations before running, since the DELETE has no soft-delete/audit trail; (3) after applying, verify `select * from platform_ai_settings` shows exactly one is_active=true row with status='valid', and `select count(*) from user_integrations where provider in ('anthropic','openai','deepseek')` returns 0; (4) if the active row isn't valid, immediately set one via the admin UI to close the AI-dark window. See D20 for the full file list.

known_issues.title: DOCX upload fails with ERR_TIMED_OUT to Supabase Storage
known_issues.workaround: Convert DOCX to PDF before uploading. PDF path fully functional.

known_issues.title: GPT-5* family forced to temperature=1 → noisier output
known_issues.detail: OpenAI enforces temperature=1 on gpt-5* (HTTP 400 otherwise). We strip temperature so call succeeds; user trades determinism for capability. Prefer Anthropic Sonnet 4.6.

known_issues.title: Jora adapter hangs Chromium on 512MB Fly worker — disabled
known_issues.detail: Commented out in backend/worker/src/sources/index.ts. Fix: (A) fly scale memory 1024; (B) rewrite to HTTP/SSR parse; (C) drop Jora.

deferred_features.title: COMPLETED 2026-06-16: Deleted dead eval harnesses (Scope A + B)
deferred_features.detail: Writer-variant eval (Scope A, commit 99aaafe) + source-eval beta tool (Scope B, commit 57a75fd). Both unused for 3+ weeks despite ongoing work. Removed ~2000 LOC, Redis worker, BullMQ queue, Supabase table writes. Recovery: git history + branches feat/writers-eval-beta and feat/source-eval-beta.

deferred_features.title: Tier 4 user-initiated JD URL fetch
deferred_features.status: built, not wired — no API route calls it
deferred_features.to_activate: Create POST /api/jobs/scrape-url. Wire to 'Fetch from URL' button on JobEditModal.

deferred_features.title: Multi-source shared jobs pool for scale (1000s of users)
deferred_features.status: designed, not built — current per-user live fetch is fine for 10-20 test users
deferred_features.reconsider: When approaching 50-100 daily active users or source rate-limit errors appear.

deferred_features.title: Split oversized service files (1k+ lines)
deferred_features.status: watch list — refactor opportunity, no behaviour change required
candidates: backend/api/app/services/eval/enforce_w3.py (1139 lines — VERIFY DEAD: production writer is w8_verified; w3 may be deletable entirely)
backend/api/app/services/eval/role_families.py (706 lines — extract role-family configs to JSON)
deferred_features.when_to_act: When touching one of these files for an unrelated reason, or when a feature needs to add to it. Do NOT do a big-bang split — high regression risk.
